Hello Dear One,
A few months ago I did a podcast about why the OI\LY purpose of a website
(direct response marketing website, at least) should be to build a list - and that anything
that detracts from that should be left out. This (like a lot of other things I do)puts me in a
minority of about 5 people on üe planet. But in my experience, putting anything on a
site that distracts from the opt-n (like social media share buttons, outbound links to other
sites, sending people to YouTube or some other place you have content) is going to take
away from opt-ins. And, if you're using email as your prime way of driüng sales, then it
makes no sense to not do everything in your power to put as many (qualified)people on
your list as possible - including by making it so they have no other option to do much of
anything else but opt-in. Another thing that puts me in the minority in Internet marketing
land is, I don't care how long people spend on my site. If anything, I want them to spend
less time. I want them to opt-in as soorr as possible and then go about their business.
Because once I get that opt-in, their ass belongs to me until they opt-out, exponentially
increasing my chances of making the sale. Ary*uy, to promote that particular podcast, I
sent an email to my list saying that I believe the ONLY real purpose of a website is to

If They're In Heaven,
Cast Thern Down Into Hell!
One of the things I taught on stage at "Email Players" subscriber Buck Rizü's
"Health Profits" Summit was this idea of, if your list is in heaven, you should cast them
down into hell. By that I mean, they are on your list because they have a problem that
needs solüng. Or a desire they want fulfilled. In either case, they haven't bought yet. And
there's a good chance they haven't bought yet because they aren't motivated enough to
buy. And, if that is the case, you have to do like the old life insurance sales trainer (forget
his name) said to do: "You have to make them see the hurst pulling up to the curb." i.e. if
they are in heaven and comfy, you take their halo, pluck out their wings, and cast them
down into the abyss! Yes, right in your emails. (And other sales materials.) For example:

editorial staff about email, storytelling, and infotainment (the recordings are now part of
the Agora Thpes product for sale only to elBenbosApprentice.com members). A.y*uy,

said he would look one of my ads over for compliafice) etc since their lawyers are, from
what I hear, especially paranoid (which is a good thing as far as I'm concerned). So he
read my ad (and mind you - this is a younger guy in his mid to upper 20's, not normally

do with acidity, especially when r tried eating a rittle baking soda
